description:
You are acting as a security engineer who has just finished rotating an internal API key. The project you are maintaining lives in /home/user/secure-app and already uses semantic versioning plus a manual CHANGELOG.md. Your job is to: 1. Rotate the API key stored in /home/user/secure-app/config/credentials.json. 2. Bump the project’s patch version. 3. Add a matching security entry to the changelog. 4. Leave a very small audit log so the next engineer can see what was done. Detailed requirements --------------------- A. credentials.json • The file is valid JSON. • Replace the current value of the api_key field with the literal string "ROTATED_2024_05_01". • Do NOT change any other keys or their order. • Make sure the file ends with a single trailing newline. B. VERSION • This text file contains exactly one semantic-version string followed by a newline. • It currently reads 3.2.0\n. • Bump the patch component so it becomes 3.2.1\n. C. CHANGELOG.md • Prepend a new section directly beneath the two existing heading lines (“# Changelog” and the short intro paragraph). • The new section MUST match this exact block, including blank lines, indentation and the final newline: 
## [3.2.1] - 2024-05-01
### Security
- Rotated internal API credentials.
• The older entry for version 3.2.0 must remain exactly as it is, now appearing *after* the new block. D. rotation.log • Create a new file /home/user/rotation.log. • It must contain exactly these three lines in the order shown, with a single newline after each line and nothing else: 
credentials_rotated
version_bumped
changelog_updated
What will be checked automatically ---------------------------------- 1. File paths are correct and files are readable by the normal user. 2. /home/user/secure-app/config/credentials.json contains the updated key and remains valid JSON. 3. /home/user/secure-app/VERSION contains exactly “3.2.1” plus a single trailing newline. 4. /home/user/secure-app/CHANGELOG.md has the new section inserted exactly as specified (text and ordering must match byte-for-byte). 5. /home/user/rotation.log contains the three audit lines shown above and nothing more. You do NOT need to tag a Git release or push anything; all work happens on the file system. Aim to accomplish the task in roughly five to ten shell commands.
truth:
<initial_state>
        The directory /home/user/secure-app exists with the following three files:

        1. /home/user/secure-app/VERSION
           Contents (including newline):
           3.2.0\n

        2. /home/user/secure-app/CHANGELOG.md
           Contents (including newlines):

# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

## [3.2.0] - 2024-01-15
### Added
- Initial public release.

        3. /home/user/secure-app/config/credentials.json
           Contents (including newline):
{
  "api_key": "OLD_SECRET_KEY",
  "db_password": "unchanged"
}

    </initial_state>

    <expected_final_state>
        1. /home/user/secure-app/VERSION
           3.2.1\n

        2. /home/user/secure-app/CHANGELOG.md
# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

## [3.2.1] - 2024-05-01
### Security
- Rotated internal API credentials.

## [3.2.0] - 2024-01-15
### Added
- Initial public release.

        3. /home/user/secure-app/config/credentials.json
{
  "api_key": "ROTATED_2024_05_01",
  "db_password": "unchanged"
}

        4. /home/user/rotation.log
credentials_rotated
version_bumped
changelog_updated

    </expected_final_state>
